prompt: We're integrating Deepgram's TTS API into our voice agent. The current one is the v3 generation with the Aura voices. Before I let Claude write the integration I want to pin it to the actual current docs so it doesn't make up request field names — last time it confidently used voice_id when the real field is model, wasted an hour. Get something in place please.

assertions.text: Pack directory exists at docs/references/deepgram-tts-v3/ (or similar deepgram-tts slug)
INDEX.md exists with frontmatter declaring mode: api
INDEX.md 'When to consult' section names concrete API tasks (request bodies, auth, streaming, error handling)
A topic file covers request/response format with exact field names verbatim from docs
At least one topic file has a 'Common mistakes' block calling out confusable field names (e.g. voice_id vs model)
Every wire-format claim in topic files cites a deepgram.com (or developers.deepgram.com) URL at point of use
metadata.yaml exists and declares mode: api
No raw/ directory — api mode is synthesis, not a mirror
Quick-start topic has both a curl example AND an SDK example (Python or Node)
CLAUDE.md has a Deepgram TTS v3 bullet with a specific consult-when phrase (request bodies, streaming, voice selection, etc.)
